reasoning.typography: IBM Plex Sans/Serif is a strong, confident choice with personality beyond Inter. The monospace date stamps create intentional rhythm. System shows discipline but isn't groundbreaking.
reasoning.hierarchy: Clear dominance of the chronological list, good date/title pairing. Navigation is appropriately subdued. Could push headline scale further but fundamentally sound.
reasoning.distinctiveness: The dark minimal aesthetic with chronological spine feels personal-blog-specific, but the structure itself (reverse-chron archive) could apply to many writers. The 'retro-80s' easter egg adds character.
reasoning.color: Exceptional restraint. Near-monochrome with subtle warm grays on black. No decorative color pollution. Accent appears only in hover states. Disciplined.
reasoning.density: Confident use of vertical space. Generous line-height in list, not afraid of emptiness. The minimal chrome and centered column show restraint. Balanced without feeling sparse.
reasoning.voice: Navigation is clean 'Selected/Archive/About/Subscribe' which is somewhat stencil, but the content-first chronological presentation and the tagline 'programming, philosophy, history, internet, startups' establishes voice. Structure serves essays well.

winning_patterns: Severe color restraint — nearly monochrome palette with warm undertones
Date stamps as left-aligned tabular numerals create clear scan rhythm
Confident negative space around centered content column
Typography pairing (IBM Plex) has more character than default system fonts
Content-first: no hero graphics, testimonials, or marketing fluff
anti_patterns: Navigation structure is still the 'Selected/Archive/About' stencil pattern
Could push typographic scale/contrast further to create more drama
The list format is safe — no experimentation with layout or entry points
Tagline in italics feels like a default styling choice
No visual hook beyond minimalism — relies entirely on content titles
